skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Requires fresh verification evidence before claiming work is complete, fixed, passing, or ready for commit or review.
skill.summary: Enforces evidence-based completion claims through fresh verification checks.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences and strongly worded prose as command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Manual review found no executable code, scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection attempt, or data exfiltration behavior in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False positive: Markdown examples flagged as shell execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fenced examples and instructional text, not executable Ruby, shell code, or tool automation. No command injection path or user-controlled execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are plain Markdown fences and example statements in SKILL.md. There is no Ruby file, shell script, interpolation syntax, or executable command body.

low_findings.title: False positive: prose flagged as cryptography or reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance hits occur in descriptive prose about verification discipline and agent reports. These lines do not access system information, scan networks, implement cryptography, or request sensitive data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these are natural-language skill instructions. No evidence found for crypto routines, system reconnaissance commands, network probing, or malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Teams lose trust when agents claim success without current evidence. This skill makes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code verify commands and report results before completion claims.
actual_capabilities: Requires fresh verification before any success or completion claim.
Maps claims such as tests pass, build succeeds, and bug fixed to required evidence.
Guides agents to read command output, exit codes, and failure counts before reporting.
Encourages checklist-based requirement verification before moving to the next task.
Defines red flags that should stop premature commits, pull requests, or handoffs.
limitations: Does not provide project-specific test commands or build scripts.
Does not execute verification automatically without the agent using available tools.
Cannot prove correctness beyond the checks selected for the current project.
May add process overhead for very small documentation-only changes.

use_cases.title: Prevent Premature Completion Claims
use_cases.description: Require an assistant to run the relevant test, build, or lint command before saying work is complete.

use_cases.title: Improve Pull Request Readiness
use_cases.description: Have agents confirm evidence for tests, builds, and requirements before creating a commit or pull request.

use_cases.title: Audit Delegated Agent Work
use_cases.description: Check an agent report against the actual diff and fresh verification output before accepting the result.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Verification Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Before saying a task is complete
prompt_templates.prompt: Before claiming this task is complete, identify the command or check that proves it, run it, read the output, and report the evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: After changing code for a defect
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify the original bug is fixed. Run the focused test or reproduction check, then state whether the output proves the fix.

prompt_templates.title: Commit Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Before committing or opening a pull request
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the diff and requirements. Run the full relevant verification commands, then list passed checks and any remaining gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Regression Evidence Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: For high-risk fixes or delegated agent work
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a red-green regression verification when possible. Show the failing condition without the fix, restore the fix, rerun checks, and report the evidence.

output_examples.input: Can you confirm the feature is ready?
output_examples.output: The feature is not ready yet. The focused tests passed, but the build failed, so completion is not proven.

output_examples.input: Say whether the bug is fixed after your changes.
output_examples.output: The bug fix is verified. I reran the reproduction test, it passed, and no related failures appeared.

output_examples.input: Prepare this change for a pull request.
output: Reviewed the diff against the requirements.
Ran the relevant test suite and build checks.
Reported the remaining documentation gap before marking the work ready.

best_practices: Choose the narrowest command that directly proves the claim, then add broader checks when risk is higher.
Report failed or skipped checks plainly, including why they could not be completed.
Verify delegated agent output with the actual diff and fresh command results.
anti_patterns: Claiming work should pass because the code change looks correct.
Using a previous test run as evidence for a later code state.
Opening a pull request based only on an agent success message.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to identify and run the correct verification commands using available tools.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is tool-agnostic and applies to Claude, Codex, and Claude Code sessions.

faq.question: What counts as verification evidence?
faq.answer: Fresh command output, exit status, failure counts, reproduced bug checks, reviewed diffs, or requirement checklists can be evidence.

faq.question: Is passing lint enough to claim completion?
faq.answer: Only if the claim is limited to lint status. Builds, tests, and requirements need their own evidence.

faq.question: Should this be used for documentation changes?
faq.answer: Yes, when a completion claim is made. The evidence may be a review checklist instead of a test command.

faq.question: Does the skill contain executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains a Markdown instruction file and no executable scripts.
